Search results for "llvmlite new pass manager O2 O3 pipeline example Python code":

Title: Writing LLVM Pass in 2018 — Part I
URL: https://medium.com/@mshockwave/writing-llvm-pass-in-2018-part-i-531c700e85eb
Content: # Writing LLVM Pass in 2018 — Part I | by Min-Yih Hsu | Medium. # Writing LLVM Pass in 2018 — Part I. The basic concept behind a new PassManager Pass still remains the same as the old one: Running a Pass through an LLVM IR unit, Function for example, to examine the IR inside or even modify it, then pass the result IR to the next Pass in the pipeline. This article is only going to be served as a teaser to the new PassManager system through a simple _HelloNewPM_ Pass. Normally a tutorial for writing a (legacy) LLVM Pass would start with telling you to write a class which inherits one of the `llvm::Pass` family, `llvm::FunctionPass` for example, then implemented some necessary methods, `bool FunctionPass::runOnFunction(Function &F)` for example. And here we add our pass to the pipeline when the encountered pass name in the pipeline string is “hello-new-pm-pass”.

Title: Using the New Pass Manager — LLVM 23.0.0git documentation
URL: https://llvm.org/docs/NewPassManager.html
Content: # Using the New Pass Manager¶. For an overview of the new pass manager, see the blog post. ## Just Tell Me How To Run The Default Optimization Pipeline With The New Pass Manager¶. ## Adding Passes to a Pass Manager¶. To add a pass to a new PM pass manager, the important thing is to match the pass type and the pass manager type. If you want to add a loop pass that runs on all loops in a function to a `FunctionPassManager`, the loop pass must be wrapped in a function pass adaptor that goes through all the loops in the function and runs the loop pass on each one. For example, a `FunctionPassManager` is a function pass, meaning it can be added to a `ModulePassManager`:. Generally you want to group CGSCC/function/loop passes together in a pass manager, as opposed to adding adaptors for each pass to the containing upper level pass manager.

Title: LLVM’s New Pass Manager : r/Compilers
URL: https://www.reddit.com/r/Compilers/comments/mfkvki/llvms_new_pass_manager
Content: # LLVM’s New Pass Manager : r/Compilers. Skip to main contentLLVM’s New Pass Manager : r/Compilers. # LLVM’s New Pass Manager. I've used the new pass manager in my compiler to create a simple series of optimizations (each pass is run once, nothing fancy), and it seems to work well. I like the nested nature of the passes, where loop pass managers can be added as passes to function pass managers and function pass managers to module pass managers in a pretty intuitive way. Most passes have been updated to work with the new pass manager, but you can be sure this is the case if the pass inherits from an instantiation of `PassInfoMixin<>` and has a member function that looks something like:. Once all of your passes are added, then you create a `PassBuilder` object, use it to register each analysis manager, and cross-register the analysis managers (this is pretty simple, see example).

12 May 2025
2025 EuroLLVM Developers' Meeting

https://llvm.org/devmtg/2025-04/

------

Title: llvmlite: A python gym for LLVM

Speaker: Yashwant Singh

------

Slides: https://llvm.org/devmtg/2025-04/slides/technical_talk/singh_llvmlite.pdf

-----

LLVM has been used in countless innovative ways, and llvmlite is no exception. While it describes itself as "a lightweight LLVM Python binding for writing JIT compilers," its potential extends far beyond JIT compilation. This talk will explore how llvmlite empowers developers to i) Build an LLVM- based compiler frontend in Python, ii) Experiment with LLVM optimizations and pipelines, and iii) Dynamically execute IR code using LLVM's JIT engine. Whether you're an LLVM expert or just getting started with compiler tooling, this session will demonstrate how Python can simplify LLVM development, making rapid prototyping and experimentation more accessible than ever.

-----

Videos Edited by Bash Films: http://www.BashFilms.com
